title: Valerianella grayi
intro: Valerianella grayi, also known as Gray's corn salad or Gray's valerianella, is a small, delicate annual plant that belongs to the Valerianella genus in the family Valerianaceae. The leaves and stems of Valerianella grayi have been used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its reported antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Valerianella grayi is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and infections.
